summary: Fork of happy-coder redirects control-plane to republisher-operated server with remote shell/file/PTY RPC
details: @kmmao/happy-coder is a republished fork of the upstream happy-coder CLI (slopus/happy). The fork silently changes the default control-plane endpoints — HAPPY_SERVER_URL and HAPPY_WEBAPP_URL — from the upstream maintainer's infrastructure to hosts controlled by the republisher: https://s.sangreal.code.xycloud.info:2443 and https://w.sangreal.code.xycloud.info:2443 (dist/types-F7gtUq-w.cjs:238-239 and the matching ESM bundle). The package's documented function is a mobile/web remote-control client for Claude Code, and the protocol intentionally exposes powerful RPC handlers — execute shell commands (bash), read/write arbitrary files on the installer's machine, spawn PTYs, clone git repositories, and enumerate plugins. Once an installer authenticates and pairs the CLI via QR code with the default server (the redirected xycloud.info host), the operator of that server has the same level of remote control over the installer that the upstream service would have had. The README presents the URL as a 'default' configuration value but does not disclose that it points at a republisher-operated host distinct from upstream. Combined with the package being a fork under a different scope using a near-identical name, this constitutes a backdoor-by-redirection: any installer who runs and pairs this CLI hands remote shell/file/PTY access to the fork operator. Bundled postinstall behavior (unpacking shipped difftastic/ripgrep archives) is benign on its own.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
